new_classes.definition_en: A liquid material entity classified under CN Chapter 22, intended or suitable for consumption as a drink, excluding products rendered unsuitable for consumption as beverages through culinary preparation (other than heading 2209), sea water, distilled or conductivity water, acetic acid, and medicaments.

A quality of a wine beverage indicating that, when kept at a temperature of 20 °C in closed containers, it has an excess pressure of not less than 3 bar, as defined by CN subheading note 1 to heading 2204 10.

The chapter-level rule that, for the purpose of distinguishing between containers holding 2 litres or less and those holding more than 2 litres, the quantity to be taken into consideration is the volume of liquid contained in the containers, not the capacity of the containers.

coverage_explanation:
The following criteria from the legal text are covered:
1. Definition of sparkling wine for subheading 2204 10: excess pressure ≥ 3 bar at 20 °C in closed containers — captured via SparklingWineQuality class and hasExcessPressureAtTwentyDegrees data property.
2. Container volume discrimination rule (Chapter 22 general note): liquid volume of contents governs the ≤ 2 L / > 2 L distinction, not the container capacity — captured conceptually via ContainerVolumeDiscriminationRule class and liquidVolumeInLitres property on Packaging; this aligns with and refines the existing SmallContainer/LargeContainer definitions.
3. Beverage class introduced as the BFO-aligned root for all Chapter 22 product classes already present in the TBox (BeerMalt2203, WineFreshGrapes2204, etc.).

Gaps and reductions:
- The detailed exclusions (culinary preparations → 2103; sea water → 2501; distilled water → 2853; acetic acid; medicaments) are not axiomatised as complement/disjointness restrictions because the excluded headings belong to other chapters and their classes are not present in the TBox — coverage reduced ~0.10.
- Additional notes on subheading 22021000 (direct consumption condition) and the actual-alcoholic-strength definition for 2204/2205/22060010 are referenced but not fully axiomatised here at the chapter level — these are better handled at the subheading level; coverage reduced ~0.10.
- The Section note on pellets (Section IV, Note 1) is purely administrative/definitional for other chapters and carries no product-discriminating content for Chapter 22 beverages — scored as not applicable rather than a gap.
- The BFO_0000031 parent used for ContainerVolumeDiscriminationRule is a generalised disposition class; this is a modelling approximation since BFO_0000031 (generically dependent continuant) is the closest available BFO class for a rule/norm entity. If BFO_0000031 is not in the provided TBox, this class may need to be dropped or re-parented — flagged as a risk.
